verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço Afternoon — 16:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

best.windFit: W 15 km/h · cross-onshore · poor wind fit

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 68 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 70/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a fun-sized window for Too soft, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 51 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 73/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro may feel too soft without more push

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 58 kJ, 14 s period, lully 38/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 14s+ at high tide is usually still too heavy and walling on exposed beaches, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; 14s+ at high tide is usually still too heavy and walling on exposed beaches.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for lunch — 13:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide makes this one less convincing.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a small window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 61 kJ, 9 s period, workable consistency 55/100, and wind is the main risk.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.  Watch: spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.
ranked.shortExplanation: Supertubos is worth a quick check for lunch — 13:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is leaning more serious than easy here.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
